convergence.target: Percolation Threshold Theory x T Cell Infiltration in Solid Tumors

E1-H1.title: Voronoi Tessellation of Tumor ECM Recovers 3D Percolation Universality Class

clinical_trials.title: Trial of PXS-5505 (Pan-LOX Inhibitor) Combined With First Line Atezolizumab Plus Bevacizumab for Treating Patients With Unresectable Hepatocellular Carcinoma
clinical_trials.connection: Directly tests LOX inhibition combined with checkpoint immunotherapy. PXS-5505 inhibits all LOX family members, directly reducing collagen crosslinking (the bond occupation probability p in E1-H1). The trial rationale explicitly states LOX inhibition improves T cell migration and anti-PD-1 efficacy. This is investment in the exact mechanism E1-H1 proposes.

grants.title: Understanding the interplay between extracellular matrix topology and tumor-immune interactions: Challenges and opportunities
grants.pi: Authors from Texas A&M, Rice University, MD Anderson Cancer Center
grants.funding: NCI-supported (institutional affiliation with NCI-designated cancer centers)
grants.connection: Federally supported research explicitly framing ECM topology as the determinant of T cell immune interactions. Calls for quantitative future research on whether ECM geometry creates threshold effects for T cell access — directly the open question E1-H1 addresses.

patents.title: Prodrugs of Lysyl Oxidase Inhibitors
patents.assignee: Institute of Cancer Research Royal Cancer Hospital
patents.connection: Patent explicitly claims LOX inhibitor prodrugs for cancer, and the specification states: 'LOX reduction reduces ECM content and tumor stiffness leading to improved T cell migration and increased efficacy of anti-PD-1 blockade.' The claim that LOX inhibition directly enhances immunotherapy by improving T cell migration supports the E1-H1 mechanism (bond percolation density control via LOX).

patents.title: Inhibitors of Lysyl Oxidases
patents.connection: Claims LOX family inhibitors for cancer treatment; sensitization to chemotherapy documented. Does not claim immunotherapy combination but establishes LOX inhibitor IP landscape.

partial_confirmations.claim: Collagen crosslinking (LOX-mediated) creates a physical barrier to T cell infiltration via reduced pore size
partial_confirmations.paper: Modulating extracellular matrix stiffness: a strategic approach to boost cancer immunotherapy
partial_confirmations.specificity: Confirms that T cells actively avoid denser, LOX-crosslinked matrices. T cell intratumoral motility inversely correlated to tumor stiffness with a deceleration threshold — independent confirmation of the pore-size barrier mechanism central to E1-H1.

partial_confirmations.claim: Collagen network topology (not just density) determines immune cell infiltration patterns
partial_confirmations.paper: Macrophages restrict tumor immune infiltration by controlling collagen topography (Science Immunology 2026)
partial_confirmations.specificity: Shows that 'immune cell localization in tumors can be predicted by the local topography of fibrillar collagens' and that intermingled (isotropic) vs. aligned (anisotropic) networks have fundamentally different permissiveness to T cell infiltration. This is a direct, independent confirmation that network topology — not just bulk stiffness — governs immune access, supporting E1-H1's Voronoi tessellation framework.

partial_confirmations.claim: ECM architecture creates distinct permissive/exclusionary states for T cells
partial_confirmations.paper: Sensory neurons drive immune exclusion by stimulating a dense ECM in TNBC (Cell 2026)
partial_confirmations.specificity: 2026 Cell paper demonstrating sensory neurons drive CGRP secretion, activating CAFs to deposit dense collagen, creating immune exclusion. This confirms the ECM-as-barrier hypothesis from a completely independent upstream pathway. The dense collagen = immune exclusion link is the empirical foundation of E1-H1.

partial_confirmations.claim: LOXL1 specifically restricts CD8+ T cell infiltration via ECM remodeling in colorectal cancer
partial_confirmations.paper: LOXL1 promotes tumor cell malignancy and restricts CD8+ T cell infiltration in colorectal cancer
partial_confirmations.specificity: Independent 2024 paper demonstrating a causal LOX family member - CD8 T cell infiltration relationship in a specific cancer type. Confirms the LOX-ECM-T cell axis across cancer types (not just the mouse models in Nicolas-Boluda 2021).

E1-H1.summary: Strong convergence. The NCT05109052 trial directly tests pan-LOX inhibition plus checkpoint immunotherapy (atezolizumab), with the stated rationale that LOX inhibition improves T cell migration by reducing ECM crosslinking — the exact mechanism E1-H1 proposes. The WO2024003558 patent explicitly claims this mechanism. Four independent papers (Science Immunology 2026, Cell 2026, Cell Death & Disease 2024, Cell Biology and Toxicology 2024) confirm that LOX-mediated collagen crosslinking creates T cell barriers, and network topology governs immune access. The Oncotarget 2024 review identifies ECM topology and T cell interaction as an open quantitative question — the precise gap E1-H1 fills with percolation theory.

E2-H2.title: Measuring Active-Percolation Universality Class via Two-Exponent Test

grants.title: Spatial interactions modulate tumor growth and immune infiltration
grants.funding: NIH-supported (published in NPJ Systems Biology and Applications, NCI-associated journal)
grants.connection: Uses quantitative modeling to show collagen patterns modulate immune infiltration via spatial interaction length scales. The paper tests whether T cell migration is parallel or perpendicular to collagen, measuring migration exponents — adjacent to the two-exponent MSD test that E2-H2 proposes, but using different mathematical framework (Lenia/predator-prey rather than percolation exponents).

partial_confirmations.claim: T cell migration in collagen exhibits anomalous (non-Brownian) diffusion patterns
partial_confirmations.paper: Spatial interactions modulate tumor growth and immune infiltration (NPJ Systems Biology 2024)
partial_confirmations.specificity: Quantitative modeling demonstrates that T cell migration in collagen is not simple Brownian motion — it depends on collagen fiber orientation and spatial interaction kernels. The finding that 'short-range interaction kernels provide a mechanism for tumor cell survival' implies T cell MSD scaling differs from Brownian in tumor ECM, supporting the premise of E2-H2 that anomalous diffusion exponents are measurable and meaningful.

partial_confirmations.claim: Collagen fiber alignment creates directional T cell migration biases measurable as distinct exponents
partial_confirmations.paper: Modulating tumor collagen fiber alignment for enhanced lung cancer immunotherapy via inhaled RNA (Nature Communications 2025)
partial_confirmations.specificity: Demonstrates that DDR1-mediated collagen fiber alignment creates physical barriers through a distinct mechanism from LOX crosslinking. When fiber alignment is disrupted by anti-DDR1 scFv, T cell infiltration improves. This confirms that collagen network anisotropy creates measurably different T cell transport regimes, supporting the E2-H2 hypothesis that isotropic vs. directed percolation produces distinguishable MSD exponents.

E2-H2.summary: Moderate convergence. No direct clinical trials test percolation universality class measurement, as expected for a basic biophysics protocol. However, two independent 2024-2025 papers confirm that T cell migration in tumor ECM is non-Brownian and that collagen topology creates distinct measurable transport regimes — the empirical premise underlying E2-H2's measurement framework. The gap between passive and active percolation universality classes (isotropic vs. directed) is not directly addressed by any paper found, confirming this remains a genuinely novel experimental question.

E4-H8.title: TGF-beta Correlated Percolation p_c Shift Predicts LOX + Anti-TGF-beta Synergy

clinical_trials.title: A Phase 1 Study of PLN-101095 (integrin avb8/avb1 inhibitor) in Adults With Advanced or Metastatic Solid Tumors (+ pembrolizumab combination)
clinical_trials.status: active/recruiting — Phase 1 dose escalation, interim data reported Dec 2025
clinical_trials.intervention: PLN-101095 (oral integrin αvβ8/αvβ1 inhibitor blocking TGF-β activation) + pembrolizumab (anti-PD-1)
clinical_trials.connection: PLN-101095 blocks integrins αvβ6/αvβ8, which activate latent TGF-β in the ECM. By blocking TGF-β activation, it disrupts TGF-β-driven LOX upregulation and correlated collagen crosslinking — the exact correlation source in E4-H8. Interim Phase 1 data (Dec 2025): 4 responses in 10 ICI-refractory patients including 1 CR and 3 PRs. Phase 1b expansion for NSCLC planned 2026. This is real clinical investment in the TGF-beta + ECM + immune axis.

clinical_trials.title: Trial of PXS-5505 (pan-LOX inhibitor) + Atezolizumab + Bevacizumab for HCC
clinical_trials.connection: Tests the LOX inhibition arm of the E4-H8 combination (LOX + anti-TGF-beta). Demonstrates that the LOX-immunotherapy combination is considered credible enough for Phase 1b/2 clinical investigation. Bevacizumab provides anti-VEGF (not anti-TGF-beta), so this is related but not the exact E4-H8 combination.

grants.title: TGF-β builds a dual immune barrier in colorectal cancer by impairing T cell recruitment and instructing immunosuppressive SPP1+ macrophages
grants.funding: Supported through institutional funding; published Nature Genetics Dec 2025
grants.connection: 2025 Nature Genetics paper demonstrating TGF-β creates immune exclusion through two parallel mechanisms: (1) direct T cell recruitment blockade, (2) SPP1-mediated collagen deposition creating physical barrier. This is an independent confirmation that TGF-β drives ECM-mediated immune exclusion through collagen — the causal mechanism underlying E4-H8's correlated percolation model. TGF-β inhibition sensitizes tumors to anti-PD-L1.

patents.title: Prodrugs of Lysyl Oxidase Inhibitors (Institute of Cancer Research)
patents.connection: Claims LOX inhibitor prodrugs noting combination with immunotherapy rationale. The LOX + immunotherapy combination is the first component of the E4-H8 dual mechanism prediction.

partial_confirmations.claim: TGF-beta drives correlated collagen deposition around CAFs, creating spatially clustered crosslink density
partial_confirmations.paper: TGF-β builds a dual immune barrier in colorectal cancer (Nature Genetics 2025)
partial_confirmations.specificity: Mechanistically confirms that TGF-β drives SPP1+ macrophage-mediated collagen deposition, creating a correlated spatial pattern of ECM reinforcement. This is the correlation source in E4-H8: TGF-β signaling from CAFs at ~50 um spacing creates correlated bond reinforcement in the percolation network. The paper also shows TGF-β inhibition rescues T cell infiltration — direct support for the combination therapy prediction.

partial_confirmations.claim: Anti-TGF-beta + checkpoint combination exceeds single-agent immunotherapy in ECM-dense tumors
partial_confirmations.paper: Multiple papers confirming anti-TGF-β + anti-PD-L1 synergy (review literature 2024-2025)
partial_confirmations.journal: PMC12061708 (Recent advances in TGF-β inhibitors in cancer)
partial_confirmations.specificity: Multiple independent reviews confirm that TGF-β inhibition + checkpoint blockade is synergistic in desmoplastic tumors. The combination produces complete responses in mouse models where monotherapy fails. This confirms the qualitative prediction of E4-H8 (combination exceeds single agent) without yet having the percolation-derived quantitative prediction of 1.2-1.5x Bliss independence.

partial_confirmations.claim: Integrin αvβ6/αvβ8-mediated TGF-β activation drives collagen crosslinking and immune exclusion
partial_confirmations.paper: Integrin-αV-mediated activation of TGF-β regulates anti-tumour CD8 T cell immunity and response to PD-1 blockade (Nature Communications 2021)
partial_confirmations.specificity: Demonstrates that integrin αvβ6/αvβ8-mediated TGF-β activation suppresses CD8 T cell immunity and resistance to PD-1 blockade. This is the molecular bridge for E4-H8: TGF-β activation (via integrins on CAFs) triggers LOX upregulation (through Smad3), which creates correlated collagen crosslinking around TGF-β-producing cells. The PLN-101095 trial operationalizes this pathway.

E4-H8.summary: Strong convergence. The PLN-101095 Phase 1 trial (NCT06270706) is active and testing TGF-β activation blockade combined with checkpoint immunotherapy, with responses in ICI-refractory patients reported Dec 2025. This trial operationalizes the TGF-β inhibition arm of the E4-H8 prediction. The 2025 Nature Genetics paper independently confirms TGF-β drives correlated collagen deposition (SPP1-macrophage axis) that excludes T cells — the physical basis for E4-H8's correlated percolation model. The prediction that LOX + anti-TGF-β produces synergy greater than either alone is directionally supported, though the quantitative percolation-derived prediction (1.2-1.5x Bliss independence) remains untested.

E3-H4.title: Michaelis-Menten LOX Kinetics and MMP Turnover Determine the p(BAPN dose) Mapping

clinical_trials.title: Trial of PXS-5505 (pan-LOX inhibitor) + Atezolizumab + Bevacizumab
clinical_trials.connection: This trial includes LOX inhibitor dose escalation (Phase 1b primary endpoint: safety/tolerability at multiple doses), generating exactly the kind of dose-response data needed to test E3-H4's predicted 0.3-0.8 mg/mL therapeutic window. The trial uses a more potent selective LOX inhibitor (PXS-5505, IC50 <40 nM for LOXL2 vs. BAPN IC50 3-8 uM), so direct BAPN dosing comparison is not possible, but the pharmacological principle is being tested.

clinical_trials.title: A Phase I/IIa Trial of PXS-5505 in Advanced Myelofibrosis
clinical_trials.connection: Established safety/PK profile for pan-LOX inhibitor amsulostat. Published in Blood 2025 (PMID 40241543). Maximum safe dose 200 mg BID with robust systemic LOX activity reduction. Provides PK data relevant to estimating in vivo LOX inhibition thresholds — the key parametric input E3-H4 needs.

patents.title: Prodrugs of Lysyl Oxidase Inhibitors
patents.assignee: Institute of Cancer Research Royal Cancer Hospital
patents.connection: Patent application for LOX inhibitor prodrugs with improved oral bioavailability. The development of prodrugs to optimize oral LOX inhibitor exposure confirms that dosing optimization for in vivo LOX activity is a recognized challenge — supporting E3-H4's argument that a percolation therapeutic window exists and must be identified pharmacokinetically.

partial_confirmations.claim: Pan-LOX inhibition at defined doses reduces desmoplasia and improves immune infiltration in pancreatic cancer
partial_confirmations.paper: A first-in-class pan-lysyl oxidase inhibitor impairs stromal remodeling and enhances gemcitabine response and survival in pancreatic cancer (Nature Cancer 2023)
partial_confirmations.specificity: PXS-5505 at specific doses reduces chemotherapy-induced desmoplasia and stiffness, improving treatment response and survival in pancreatic cancer models. This provides preclinical dose-response data for pan-LOX inhibition and establishes that a dose-dependent ECM normalization effect exists — the empirical basis for E3-H4's p(dose) function. The paper did not test a percolation window, but confirms the dose-dependent pore-size normalization that is E3-H4's key prediction.

partial_confirmations.claim: LOX inhibitor PK in humans establishes in vivo dosing parameters
partial_confirmations.paper: A phase I/IIa trial of PXS-5505 in advanced myelofibrosis (Blood/PubMed 2025)
partial_confirmations.specificity: First human PK/PD data for a pan-LOX inhibitor. 200 mg BID safe with robust systemic LOX reduction. This human PK data is the first empirical constraint on in vivo LOX inhibition levels — allowing a more principled estimate of the IC50_apparent in vivo than the 80 uM parametric estimate in E3-H4 (which QG flagged as discrepant). The human data would allow E3-H4's kinetic model to be recalibrated.

E3-H4.summary: Moderate convergence. The NCT05109052 trial tests pan-LOX inhibitor dose escalation plus immunotherapy — generating data directly relevant to E3-H4's dose-response prediction, though using a more potent inhibitor than BAPN. The myelofibrosis Phase I/IIa trial (PMID 40241543) provides the first human LOX inhibitor PK data, enabling principled recalibration of E3-H4's in vivo IC50_apparent. The Nature Cancer 2023 PXS-5505 paper confirms dose-dependent desmoplasia reduction, supporting the existence of a therapeutic window. However, no trial directly tests BAPN dose-response for T cell infiltration, and the percolation framework for interpreting the window remains novel.
